column36: Finally got to unpack my Fear Itself :D

After a great Game 1, I had a huge write up for it. I didn’t expect Game 2 to be even better. One of my fastest Legendary games! I am quickly becoming a huge fan of Blade, Daywalker’s Hunt High And Low! I got 2 early copies on HYDRA team and with the Hivelings, thinned the deck quickly. SHIELD team got an early rare, Silent Meditation, and used it to great effect, making for some big time turns recruiting directly to hand. Iron Fist was amazing as always. Hiroim was amazing for me in Game 1, and merely “very good” Game 2. Seemed like the no brainer pick. This game was nearly a loss with an early run of Scheme Twists, but we stabilized and dominated the rest of the way.

This was arguably the best game we've ever finished at, definitely one of the best. Our first game actually went really well, but the second was was better. Colossus and Wolverine rounded out the heroes greatly. Fun game!

Great setup for us. Challenging in interesting ways and gave us some strong heroes to work with. The card draw and KO was strong and so adding Wolverine in the second attempt unlocked this setup. I focused on Wolverine, my better half on Colossus and we were able to generate consistent 10+ attack by the end.

column2: That's at Least a Six on the Rictor Scale
column36:
Got very lucky the first game. Never had a scheme twist till turn 8, had Sinister Henchmen early that got the Strong Guy Investigate card cloned. Thing 5 drop filtered my recruit into attack to fight things early.

Shatterstar and Richtor was a natural blend being brothers. Though the idea and plan for both decks was to use "Investigate" as a form of card draw to benefit selection and getting attack early to weed out enemies so it was smooth sailing. Could have finished a turn or two earlier but made it a point to try and never have more than three villains in the city at a time.

Even with proper planning, close to getting an escape on multiple turns but those extra Bystanders from Sinister popped up to stop things from getting pushed or revealed from the Annihilation Wave reveal effect.

I really like this setup design. A lot w/ the city, considering the scheme, Annihilation Wave, and Subterranea. Plus the Sinister clones could push through quick. Felt very fun. Games went well, not sure if I just got lucky and had way too strong heroes or if it was the setup not being that hard. I think, it felt too easy, which is my only complaint. But, also, Doom 2099 just seems really good. And with Valeria and Rictor helping draw cards, he can go crazy. First game was a bit better, but basically the same, and I'm concerned I may have forgotten a time or two to play the extra villian card after tide rushes out. I'm not the best at proxying, I guess. Hopefully I didn't do anything wrong.

The concept of this one is fun and the enemies really synergize well to make this an extremely difficult scenario that can steamroll quickly. Really diabolical to have Annihilation wave and a Cloning Henchmen group on a scheme that will often be a small city. That said the default heroes and the sets allowed didn't make it as fun as I think it could have been.

Doom 2099, Rictor, and Brainstorm worked so well together, with Doom Blast powered up to max strength very quickly and solid deck thinning. The strength deck on the other side just never came together. Tsunami waves plus cloning Mr. Sinisters are not a great combination!

Very brutal setup with the escape scheme, small city, and villain amount via clone and annihilation wave. Even with using human torch for early attack, couldn't get things under control enough in either game

Tough luck in these games! First game had so many henchmench and villains, second game I gained many wounds in just a few turns and couldn't recover from Galactus escaping. I regret blowing 2 pretty good heroes that didn't take off in this pod!

First loss, Galactus ate a ton of the city, floods kept escaping him and he kept shuffling up top.  Brutal 16 turns.  Second loss was just a never ending stream of Sinister clones (and Galactus munched on the low tide land) and really bad hand draws.  I wanted to like this setup, but it was just rough.  Still not a 1, as I wanted it work for me.  (Attempt 3 went 19 rounds and felt some progress, but still a loss).

You lose if you have 6 villains escape... Let's start out my making the city smaller. Oh and Mr. Sinister wants to clone himself. What's that Valture 2099 make Villains escape too! Oh and our Friend Baktar boots someone from the streets. This was tough.

column2: The Kree to Escaping: That Guy's Prostetic Leg
column36: Heavy Gamora (Hand 2)/Deathlok (Hand 1) game but the true MVP were 2 early game Incoming Detectors for Hand 1. The constant stream of Ambush Henchmen and Villains gave a major boost to controlling the City and keeping the number of Shards on the MM manageable.

The first game went well but I was reckless with the city trying to get the turn count low. Lots of shatterstar made it a relatively easy win. The second game I got the early moon knight KO card and almost ran the deck into the ground. I ended up buying 4 officers so I would be able to continue recruiting. The deck ended with only 10 cards and no starter cards. Lots of interesting interactions with the villains and the facedown HQ. My only complaint is the lack of bystander variety leading to a lot of basic bystanders but that's a small complaint.

column36: it was ok. It was really annoying having to pay to turn over the heroes. I thought it would be a cool concept but it just ended up being a pain in the butt. Probably wouldn't play this scheme again if it were up to me.

It was great to try a scheme I did not own. Went with thematic guardians setup, mainly for shard and artifact synergies. The extra villain deck showed its effect in both games, the strong villain cards (hate u Random!) resulted in 1 escape each game. Also the constant pressure led to not being able to recruit from HQ, so bought agents and sidekicks often. This in turn led to many recruits that I could use for buying neat artifacts including StarLord rare. With the more stable inflow of shards, and after stabilizing the city I could hit the Supreme Intelligence on turns 23,24,25,26 and 27; so the game sort of had nice narrative arc with strong Act 3. (Porbably would have been more fun with a different henchman group.)
Fun having to unveil the HQ before knowing what to recruit.

Player 1 had some great luck off the bat with 6 recruit in turn 1 to buy Jean Grey's 3 recruit card. Very quickly built a great deck with Jean Grey/Iceman, with a bit of support from Scarlet Witch and Silver Surfer. The final turn was epic. Locations on every city spot. The mastermind with still a tactic underneath him. And then the swordsman comes out and puts bystanders everywhere. Beautiful for this deck. Eventually drew the entire deck (and copied a few key cards along the way) for a whopping 83 attack over the course of the whole turn!

Player 2's deck with Falcon/Winter Soldier and Scarlet Witch just never really got off the ground. Bad luck with hero draws and drawing during the turn. Usually about 5 or 6 attack per turn, but still hit the mastermind once.

The setup was fine. Most of the fun was from the heroes I picked myself rather than the combo from the setup. Grim Reaper/Lethal Legion is one of the most boring sets imo. I like the scheme, but as long as you can think ahead to focus 1 each turn on behalf of your partner, it can be a little too easy.

column36: Being able to manage the villain decks really helped

choose scarlet witch to work with falcon and soldiers dark memories, and it only sorta worked, mostly because the Ange;/ Jean grey bystander nonsense just took off so hard game two, they didn't really have time, and player two kept drawing her dark memories with no discard pile
Death did not come for the multiverse.

I absolutely *hate* this scheme mostly from the setup aspect, as well as how it doesn't feel thematic to me, so that immediately knocks down at least 1 point.
When evaluating this setup, I did consider Hellcat, but I severely underestimated how much impact she would have. I focused too much on the Thunderbolts' emphasis on henchmen and the locations. So I used War Machine to both remove henchmen from the equation as well as support Jean's love of bystanders and Blade to manipulate villain placement. Big mistake. I was ultimately unable to make much progress, and eventually lost.
So for attempt 2, I figured I needed to bring Hellcat in, realizing that I could just leave a pile alone if I noticed there was a twist. Unfortunately, by the time that became relevant, I was already popping off, and half the twists had resolved by turn 6 as it was. Intimidated by how badly I failed in game 1, I brought out the nuke that is Invisible Woman, both for her amazing KO ability as well as her ability to quickly fuel up Jean Grey with bystanders. While that synergy did not present itself, Silver Surfer did get to explode quite well, especially with Hellcat's assistance.
I lament that there didn't seem to be much theme going on here, but I will admit I had a much better time on game 2 than usual. So I'm awarding a 4 mainly because I tend to award 3s, and this deserves to be considered at least a little above average.
We learned from game 1 that constantly checking the top of every deck was paramount to survival. Still barely scraped out a victory, but at least we had fun.

column36:
Went in trying to figure out how to split three Hero Classes into two player decks in a relatively balanced way. Wound up with Daredevil on one side and Ice Man on the other. Chuck got 30,000,000 votes before I was able to get anything going, so Murdock made the decision to announce his candidacy. Basically destroyed any chance of a consistent DD deck by throwing all of his cards that I could get (except his rare) out there for votes. Turned Iceman into general Ranged focus, then replaced the lost DD cards with Tech, mainly Tony. Ultimately, Matt Murdock won the presidency. 

Also, I forgot I could send grey Heroes Undercover when I put Heroes in for votes, so I made it slightly more difficult on myself.

The becoming the president scheme is one I've never come across and it was actually really fun. I thought thematically, I'd use Luke Cage noir (as the sometime mayor of NYC, and Kamala Kahn (as the future first mutant president according to the recent inglorious x-force comic).  But Daredevil was the guy who kept getting recruited and sent to run against Xavier.  I thought maybe Ms. Marvel or Viv Vision because of their size changing recruitment costs might work better, but once I had 3 or 4 Daredevils in the pile, I just started recruiting him to keep the election close.   I ended up having 8 Daredevils against 9 scheme twists at the end of the game.  That Iceman is crazy if you can stack a hand of him.  It was a blast to play.

TLDR: I did not have fun this week.


This was the worst game of legendary I may have ever played. Its a shame cause I really like CX:PoC his name alone puts a smile on my face but this scheme was just brutal. I could barely fight back. I was hemorrhaging cards. This was so bad in the first game I thought "What If....I just bring some bad but cheap heroes just to vote for?" So I brought SPN and Elektra all in all not the worse heroes in the game but with their commons having a combined total of 4 and 3 costs I figured I could get them to show up and vote quick to max out the polls. This didn't work either. I focused on SM first cause he showed up and with all 14 cards at 2 cost it was a solid plan. However, he completely spider-ghosted me after the opening line up. Both of my games were so bad I played a 3rd just for me to try and figure something out and it was also just as bad. This game has not only furthered my distaste for proxy but also encouraged me to not buy 2099. All in all I think the real problem here was the scheme if it wasn't for that I probably would have been chill. I think the scheme theme was too interesting and the mechanics were a bit under developed. I mean 11 twists and you lose at 4 if you can't fight back thats crazy.

Another tough setup. First time playing against Evil DP and his corps(e). Base 11 attack was just hard to climb to. The Corpse weren't too difficult. First time with the Rival Overlords and they were a neat challenge. Thanos escaped but just ascended. Then he immediately wrecked my hands with two strikes before I could get to him. 
First time with Captain Carter, Killmonger, Solo, and Clea. I attempted to develop a What If based deck with Clea manipulating the top of the deck. It worked decently. It ended up a mix of strength, tech, and Clea's ranged cards.
Other hand focused on recruiting 2s, 3s, and 5s. Ended up hitting MM with recruit once, and attack once, but never developed more than 12 of either. 
Both decks felt a bit wonky. But I'm trying things I havent tried before so I expect things to be that way.

Evil Deadpool is a jerk face lol. The struggle was real!!! Warpath was not any help to us at all! The scheme was a bugger. We lost all but 1 pile. How do you decide what pile to keep other than get rid of less card pile lol Henchmen made us loose all good cards everytime! Lmao. Glad its over hahah
I hate Hyper-Insane Healing Factor!
Giants hurt me game 1.  Game 2 had 6 scheme twists in the first 12 turns so that was as fun as can be imagined  For the 2 heroes I selected may have seen them twice across both games.

This was one of the most fun setups I have played in the tournaments. Darren Cross and the Hypnotize Scheme play together well. The temptation to stall into Scheme Twists for another round or two was such a good contrast to being worried about another Master Strike or possibly getting caught with a bad draw and having the Twists pile up. Thanks for a really good one. Marking this to play again in the off seasons.

A lot of pressure in the city.  Really felt like I was pushing my luck on escapes early.  Transforming Mastermind and an Ambush Scheme were both fun.  Though I kept forgetting to clear the bystanders off the scheme until another twist would hit, and then I'd think, "oh yeah" got to work on those.
I think I really chose the right hero's to make the deck work for the scheme and mastermind we were fighting. The decks really seemed to gel well and worked together to control the board.

Relatively straight forward setup. We went for Wasp and Tony to lean into Tech and thought Coordinate would be super helpful but it was only so-so as we often needed to keep the strength ourselves as there was always something to fight with the Bystanders. Hopefully we turned in the correct game as our other attempt had a higher PPT but took longer.

Crazy fun setup! The setup really allowed some heroes to crank up to 11! Especially Cassie Lang. I had such a good shuffle in my first attempt, that my crazy Cassie Lang output in my second attempt didn't even matter! (Fortunate problem to have.) She generated 5+12=17 attack for me because of all the Hypno-Thrall Villains from the setup. It seemed too good to be true, so I confirmed with Bagel in Tourney-Help that they count towards her rare. I picked Ant Army and High-Tech Spider-Man to really maximize Ultimate Spider-man because his rare is (and was for me in 1st attempt) very strong and worth building towards. High-tech Spidey's red cards were the only duds. Despite the Thor Corps' mild annoyance, they were good for enabling other cards. All the size-changing was really fun, and one deck focused on mostly black while the other did green to maximize the effects between each. Sooo fun, and I will love to play it again. Peter Parker, Homecoming was another card I considered trying.
As Chuck Norris once said in regards to Yellowjacket.....I don't initiate violence, I retaliate.

kingpin and especially black swan were great picks for the setup imo, also got a lot of value out of crystal and venom and since they all are separate teams capt britain didnt do too bad either. not rly a difficult set-up but a real fun one for sure!

column36: Very fun and challenging match! We picked Quasar and Adam Warlock to help keep the MM attack from "Ultimate Abomination" lower (most their cards do not have high attack values but rely on shard economies). This strategy generally panned out well. Danger sense from Moondragon was very helpful to help mitigate the plutonium from getting out of hand. Captain America 1941 eventually was pulling off some BIG swings near the end which felt really good!

We had a difficult time at first getting enough recruit that will have to be a focus on our second game. We need to also decide if having these high attack cards are worth it because all they do is boost Zola. Second round was an utter disaster. We had two villains escape by turn 18 and called it. That can get bad quickly.

I got stuck for so many turns being one point short of hitting the mastermind. I KO'd too many recruit cards of mine and got stuck after a master strike. I only managed to survive by cheesing sidekicks with 4 copies of Hercules' common and getting some beneficial effects.

A good challenge!

We went in for piercing energy and it worked well. We enjoy playing it for a change. Our 2nd game was only a couple of turns slower so very similar results. Great set up again, loads of fun. Thank you.

I was able to recruit the Rare Psylocke early in the game and used it later to put the Rare Banshee in the ranged focused deck's hand.

I had a lot of fun with this scheme, I like the ones that change the status quo like adding extra city spaces and I had never played this one before. My game ended up very lopsided. One hand managed to grab a rare cards fairly early on and then two more so that hand was responsible for defeating the mastermind all 5 times and ended up with 7x the score of the other "hand", a first for me.
Tracking down Hank Pym was a bit of a pain but I got the hang of it quickly especially when I pared down almost all my SHIELD starters in the "strong hand". I chose Wonder Man just because I had just seen the show and never used his cards before and his Absorb Ambient Energy synergy with Lady Thor's Chosen by Asgard was really useful. Managed to pull that off a few times. My only regret was that Stan Lee was still in the villain deck at the end of the game.

Early Mobilize for War two times baby woo! Big KO power both games but things came together easier in the first one despite the results of the 2nd being better. Burned through all of Ultron's legacy game 2 which was a funny realization.

Biggest complaint is that it felt like I only had 1 real choice to make in set up due to Agent X-13 requiring Steve Rodgers to function decently at all.
Hank Pym is always a pain to fight, but the scheme is fun.

Great setup. Went with Apocalyptic KP and Uatu for KO power to make it easier to hit Pym. Right side however, was able to pick up Kitty's rare on Turn 7, and had 4 of the 5 rares by the end. Still took longer to finish that I'd have liked, but going with turns over points on this one.

column36: Fives summiting for ReoFirestar: this game was amazing. Player 1 had loads of KO from bystanders and villains at the start which paired amazingly with Ant-Man. Also Wonder man was a necessary pick because he sent all the cards that didn't draw or KO on the bottom, which helped immensely with player 1's strategy

The first two cards of the villain deck, BOTH GAMES, were Ultimate Captain Marvel and then a scheme twist. This game we had 4 scheme twists in the first 10 turns and the first floor to go was 4 bystanders.
